sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Investors pour $60M into Ovid Therapeutics to advance seizure drugs
feed.summary: Ovid Therapeutics has secured $60 million in investment to further its work on seizure drugs, marking its second private investment in public equity (PIPE) deal in recent months. This funding highlights continued investor interest in the company's pharmaceutical developments. The article also touches on other business news, including strong Manhattan luxury home sales and changes in New York's outdoor dining policies.

feed.title: Wells Fargo upgrades Oneok stock rating on Iran war opportunities
feed.summary: Wells Fargo has upgraded Oneok Inc (NYSE:OKE) to Overweight from Equal Weight, raising its price target to $100 from $81. The upgrade is based on expectations that Oneok will exceed its 2026 guidance and 2027 consensus estimates, driven by spread-based opportunities related to the Iran war. The midstream energy company also boasts a 4.71% dividend yield and a 56-year history of dividend payments.

feed.title: Lee Johnson Capital Management LLC Purchases 5,944 Shares of Quanta Services, Inc. $PWR
feed.summary: Lee Johnson Capital Management LLC significantly increased its stake in Quanta Services, Inc. (NYSE:PWR) during the fourth quarter, purchasing 5,944 additional shares, bringing its total holdings to 8,006 shares valued at $3.379 million. The construction company recently reported strong Q4 earnings, surpassing analyst expectations for EPS and revenue, and announced a quarterly dividend. Analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" rating for Quanta Services with an average price target of $576.55, reflecting institutional investor confidence and positive financial performance.

feed.title: Lockheed Martin To Quadruple Precision Strike Missile Output After First Combat Use In Iran War
feed.summary: Lockheed Martin announced it will quadruple the production of its Precision Strike Missiles (PrSM) following a new agreement with the U.S. Department of War. This decision comes after the U.S. Central Command confirmed the PrSM's first combat use in the ongoing Iran war. The increased production builds on a previous $4.94 billion contract awarded in March 2025.

feed.title: General Motors starts tests of “eyes-off” automated driving
feed.summary: General Motors has commenced supervised public-road testing of its next-generation "eyes-off" automated driving technology on limited-access highways in California and Michigan, utilizing over 200 development vehicles. This move signifies a shift from manual data collection to active testing of automated functions, building on over a million miles of data collected previously. GM plans to introduce this capability in 2028, starting with the Cadillac ESCALADE IQ, and eventually expand it from highways to driveway-to-driveway functionality across various models.

feed.title: 20,303 Shares in First Solar, Inc. $FSLR Purchased by Lee Johnson Capital Management LLC
feed.summary: Lee Johnson Capital Management LLC recently purchased 20,303 shares of First Solar, Inc. (NASDAQ:FSLR) valued at approximately $5.3 million, making it a new significant holding for the firm. Despite this institutional accumulation, company insiders have been net sellers, offloading 75,989 shares worth about $14.9 million in the last three months. First Solar reported a mixed Q4 earnings with revenue beating estimates but EPS missing, and faces a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating along with a potential legal investigation.

feed.title: MSCI Inc. stock shows resilience amid market volatility as demand for indexes and ESG data drives steady growth
feed.summary: MSCI Inc. stock demonstrates resilience on the NYSE despite market volatility, driven by consistent demand for its index, analytics, and ESG products. The company's diversified revenue streams, high-margin subscription model, and strong analyst ratings position it as a defensive growth play for US investors. Current market performance and future projections indicate robust growth and profitability, supported by its strategic positioning and competitive advantages in the financial data sector.

feed.title: Generac (NYSE: GNRC) recasts segments, highlights $715,542 Adjusted EBITDA
feed.summary: Generac Holdings Inc. announced a reorganization of its reporting structure into Residential and Commercial & Industrial (C&I) segments, effective March 31, 2026. This change is intended to better align reporting with how leadership evaluates performance and allocates capital, without affecting historical consolidated financial results. For the twelve months ending December 31, 2025, the company reported net sales of $4,209,147 and Adjusted EBITDA of $715,542, with the Residential segment contributing the larger portion of both.

feed.title: 3 Energy ETFs Riding Oil’s Surge to 34%, 57%, and 113% Gains in 2026
feed.summary: The article details how three energy instruments – Energy Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLE), VanEck Oil Services ETF (OIH), and Permian Basin Royalty Trust (PBT) – have significantly benefited from WTI crude oil's surge. XLE offers broad energy exposure, OIH focuses on oilfield services companies and is more volatile, while PBT is a unique royalty trust providing income from aging Texas oil and gas properties, currently facing litigation. Each instrument provides different levels of risk, return, and exposure to the energy market.

feed.title: American Express Rolls Out New Business Cards to Target Growing Companies
feed.summary: American Express has launched a new Graphite Business card with a $295 annual fee, offering cashback rewards, and plans another launch later in the year, as part of its strategy to strengthen its position in the small and mid-size business market. Recognizing business clients as valuable targets due to higher spending volumes, AmEx aims to cement its competitive advantage amidst market shifts, including Capital One's recent acquisition of Brex. The company also announced a new expense management software and a $300 annual reimbursement for ChatGPT Business subscriptions for certain cardholders.

feed.title: Professional Advisory Services Inc. Grows Position in AT&T Inc. $T

This move positions AT&T as the firm's 29th-largest holding, reflecting positive institutional sentiment. Analysts currently rate AT&T as a "Moderate Buy" with a consensus price target of $30.46, following a strong Q4 earnings beat and solid FY2026 guidance.

feed.title: PDD Holdings (PDD) Stock Advances 1.91% on Strong Revenue Despite Earnings Decline
feed.summary: PDD Holdings (PDD) saw its stock advance by 1.91% despite a quarterly earnings decline, driven by strong revenue growth, particularly from transaction services. While revenue expanded by 12% year-over-year, net earnings fell by 11% due to increased operational expenditures, including significant investments in sales, marketing, and R&D. The company's long-term strategy focuses on strengthening supply chain infrastructure and ecosystem enhancement, supported by a robust cash position.

feed.title: Bank of America files plan tied to planned SouthPark retail branch
feed.summary: Bank of America has filed a land development construction plan for a proposed two-story retail branch in SouthPark, Charlotte. This development is part of a broader retail expansion strategy by the Charlotte-based bank to add more branches across the region. The article also mentions related news about BofA's other financial activities and corporate changes.

feed.title: The Home Depot, Inc. stock rises on Mingledorff's HVAC acquisition expanding pro customer reach
feed.summary: The Home Depot, Inc. announced its subsidiary SRS Distribution will acquire HVAC wholesaler Mingledorff's, targeting the $100 billion market and boosting its total addressable market to $1.2 trillion. This strategic acquisition expands Home Depot's focus on professional contractors, a high-growth, high-margin segment, to mitigate a slowdown in retail demand. Analysts view the deal as a strategic move for long-term growth, despite limited near-term financial impact, positioning Home Depot for resilient performance in a changing market.

feed.title: SF Holding Upgraded to "AA" in MSCI ESG Ratings, Reinforcing Its Global Leadership in Sustainable Logistics
feed.summary: SF Holding, the largest integrated logistics service provider in Asia, has been upgraded to an "AA" rating in the latest MSCI ESG Ratings. This places SF Holding first among the world's top four integrated logistics service providers and highlights its leadership in sustainable logistics and strong commitment to ESG practices. The upgrade reflects enhanced recognition of SF's ESG capabilities, execution, and international investor confidence in its green logistics efficiency and long-term growth.

feed.title: Lockheed Martin Answers the Nation's Call and Quadruples Precision Strike Missile Production
feed.summary: Lockheed Martin and the Department of War have formed an agreement to quadruple the production capacity of Precision Strike Missiles (PrSM), building on a previous $4.94 billion contract with the U.S. Army. This initiative aims to enhance the U.S. military's deep-strike capabilities and strengthen the industrial base, with PrSM recently used in combat for the first time. The company has invested over $7 billion since 2017 to expand its production capacity for critical systems, including munitions.
